sourceDesc.p: Derived from online catalogues of Hathi Trust, the Bodleian Library, the British Library, and the Library Hub

revisionDesc.change: Tagged in TEI and exposed on Digital Lacy website

body.p: This short bibliography lists printed works attributed to Pascoe which are now available online in order of publication date. Some of these titles are effectively serials, reappearing annually with some modification but essentially the same content. The list does not include all such re-issues. Download links are included where possible. Where a title is not available online, I have indicated its shelf mark in the British Library, or a link to its entry in the Bodleian catalogue.
Please raise an issue on the Github repository if you spot any errors or omissions.

div.head: Published reference works and guides available online

bibl.title: The prohibitory law of Massachusetts versus the licensing law of England : with an account of the latter / by Charles E. Pascoe

A London directory for American travellers for 1874. Containing the fullest information, in the best form for reference, respecting all that is valuable in connection with a visit to London. With an appendix. By Charles E. Pascoe
bibl.publisher: Boston: Lee and Shepard; Lee, Shepard, and Dillingham, 1874

bibl.title: A London Guide and Directory for American travellers ... with a large new map of London By Charles Eyre Pascoe. 1876. Fifth edition.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, and Co., Stationers' Hall Court

bibl.title: A practical handbook to the principal schools of England, based for the most part on the statutes, schemes, and regulations made under the Public Schools' Acts by the Public Schools Commissioners; ... A handy guide for the use of parents, guardians, and others interested in the education of boys.
bibl.publisher: London: Sampson Low, Marston, Searle & Rivington

bibl.title: The illustrated handbook to the supplementary art galleries of London : spring exhibitions, 1877

A practical handbook to the principal professions : compiled from authentic sources, and based on the most recent regulations concerning admission to the Navy, Army, and Civil Services (Home and Indian), the legal and medical professions, the professions of a civil engineer, architect, and artist, and the mercantile marine / by Charles Eyre Pascoe

listBibl.bibl: ;1879Schools for girls and colleges for women : a handbook of female education chiefly designed for the use of persons of the upper middle class : together with some chapters on the higher employment of women London : Hardwicke and Bogue [Reviews in Basingstoke and North Hampshire Gazette, 1879-05-03; Pall Mall Gazette, 1979-06-07; Examiner, 1879-10-18 p. 1352] CUP reprint

bibl.title: Dramatic notes : an illustrated handbook of the London theatres 1879
bibl.publisher: London: David Bogue: 3, St. Martin's Place, Trafalgar Square.

bibl.title: The dramatic list; a record of the principal performances of living actors and actresses of the British stage. With criticisms from contemporary journals. Comp. and ed. by Charles Eyre Pascoe
bibl.note: [Reviews: Morning Post, 1878-12-27; Boston Evening Transcript, 1879-01-18; The Athenaeum, 1879-04-12, no. 2685, pp. 483-484 (by Joseph Knight) The Era, 1879-12-21]

bibl.title: The dramatic list : a record of the performances of living actors and actresses of the British stage. Edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe. Second edition, revised and enlarged.
Where shall I educate my son? A manual for parents of moderate means / By Charles Eyre Pascoe
London of to-day : an illustrated handbook for the season
London of to-day : an illustrated handbook for the season
bibl.publisher: London: Sampson, Low, Marston, Searle & Rivington, 1885

bibl.title: London of to-day : an illustrated handbook for the season.. Second Year of Publication, and Third Edition
bibl.publisher: London: Sampson, Low, Marston, Searle & Rivington, 1886

bibl.title: "The joyous neighbourhood of Covent Garden" : a literary souvenir of the Tavistock Hotel, done in celebration of its hundredth anniversary / by Charles Eyre Pascoe. The illus. contributed by John Jellicoe
London of to-day; an illustrated handbook for the season... The Fourth Annual Edition, completely revised, with numerous additional illustrations
London of to-day; an illustrated handbook for the season 1889... The Fifth Annual Edition, with numerous additional illustrations
Brighton of to-day : an illustrated book for all seasons, but more particularly those of 1889 / by Charles Eyre Pascoe

Everyday life in our public schools : sketched by head-scholars of Eton, Winchester, Westminster, Shrewsbury, Harrow, Rugby, Charterhouse. To which is added a brief notice of St. Paul's and Merchant Taylors' schools, and Christ's hospital ; with a glossary of some words in common use in those schools / edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe; with 24 illustrations
bibl.publisher: London: Griffith and Farran ; E.P. Dutton, [188-?]

bibl.title: London of to-day. An illustrated handbook for the season 1890... The sixth Annual Edition, revised and in part rewritten, and comprising numerous additional illustrations.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Kent, & Co., Ld., and all booksellers. 1890

bibl.title: London of to-day an illustrated handbook for the season 1891... The Seventh Annual Edition, revised and in part rewritten, and comprising numerous additional illustrations.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Kent, & Co., Ld., and all booksellers. 1891

bibl.title: Brighton; a pocket-book of local information for visitors. Illustrated by Holland Tringham and W. Luker, jun
The American roads through England: An Illustrated Pocket-book of Matter-of-fact Information; ... (Special Edition for use of visitors at the Adelphi hotel, Liverpool)
bibl.publisher: London: Hazell, Watson, and Viney, Ld.

bibl.title: The American roads through England; a pocket-book of matter-of-fact information. Compiled for the use of American travellers
bibl.publisher: London: Hazell, Watson, and Viney, Ld.

bibl.title: The roads to Paris, from London: A Pocket-book for those who are unfamiliar with them. Compiled by Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by H. Tringham.
bibl.publisher: London: Hazell, Watson, and Viney, Ld.

bibl.title: London of today: an illustrated handbook for the season, 1892... The Eighth Annual Edition, revised and in large part re-written, and comprising additional Illustrations.
bibl.publisher: London: Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Kent, & Co., Ld., and all booksellers 1892

bibl.title: London of To-day. An illustrated handbook for the season, 1893... The Ninth Annual Edition, revised and in large part re-written, and comprising additional Illustrations.
bibl.note: The Quarterly Review vol 182 (1895) No. 363, art III, pp 59-82 : this impressive essay, though ostensibly referring to this title, along with Booth's Life and Labour of the People in London, does not explicitly discuss either work.

listBibl.bibl: `1893An illustrated souvenir of Victoria House, the head-quarters of the Royal commission for Great Britain at the World's Columbian exposition, Chicago, 1893London: Johnstone, Norman & Co.

bibl.title: London's world's fair for 1893 and Great Social, Politgical, and Moral Exposition. The Spirit of the Age at a glance. Explained by Charles Eyre Pascoe and Illustrated by Fred. Pegram
bibl.publisher: London: Sampson Low, Marston & Company Limited.

bibl.title: London of To-day; an illustrated handbook for the season 1897. The Thirteenth Yearly Edition. Shilling souvenir edition, sixtieth year of the Queen's Reign.
bibl.publisher: London: Bemrose & sons Limited... and at the Office of "London of To-Day," Hastings House, Norfolk Street, Strand, W.C.

bibl.title: London of today, handbook for the season 1902 / by Charles Eyre Pascoe [17th edition]
bibl.publisher: London : Simpkin, Marshall, Hamilton, Adams, Kent & Co., Ltd. Paternoster Row, E.C.

bibl.title: The pageant & ceremony of the coronation of Their Majesties King Edward the Seventh and Queen Alexandra, by Charles Eyre Pascoe
bibl.note: [Reviews: Boston Evening Transcript? 1902?; Gentlewoman and Modern Life, 1902-04-05

bibl.title: No. 10, Downing Street, Whitehall: its history and associations ... The illustrations in colour, and other sketches, by Charles E. Flower. A Memory of Great Names and an Inheritance of Great Examples.
bibl.note: Announced in New York Times 29 Jun 1907

bibl.title: York historic pageant : souvenir

The portrait book of our kings and queens, 1066-1911. Done in commemoration of the coronation of their Majesties King George V and Queen Mary. With supplementary notes on the ceremony. Edited by T. Leman Hare. The Historical and coronation Notes by Charles Eyre Pascoe
bibl.publisher: London and Edinburgh: T.C. & E.C. Jack

div.head: Pocket Guides and pamphlets not available online

bibl.title: A Summer-day's pilgrimage to Epping Forest.
Folkestone of to-day : an illustrated book for visitors; season 1889 / By Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by H. Tringham.
Eastbourne, Hastings, and St. Leonard's : a pocket-book of local information for visitors
The London Season. The monthly edition of “London of To-Day.” NS. vol. 3. no. 25-27. May-July 1897.
Descriptive and pictorial account of Cromer, Sheringham, Mundesley, and the Broads
Descriptive and pictorial account of Scarborough, Whitby, Filey and Bridlington

listBibl.bibl: 1898Holiday Leaflets Illustrated. Great Northern Series ... Written and arranged by C. E. Pascoe, etc. no. 1-5.London: Office of 'London of To-Day'BL 10347.e.48. (Contents: Three Days in the Dukeries; Cromer, Sheringham, Mondesley, and the Broads; A trip into Lincolnshire: Skegness, Mablethorpe, Sutton-on-Sea, and Woodhall Spa; An excursion into Yorkshire: Scarborough, Whitby, Filey, and Bridlington; Some Northern Health Resorts: Harrogate, Ilkley, and Ben Rhydding) BL Historical Print Editions

bibl.title: The Golf Grounds of the South-west: A Gossiping Summary of Their Various Attractions
bibl.publisher: Published at the office of "London of To-Day,"

bibl.title: Twelfth night: a plea for an old custom / (written by Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by John Jellicoe)

listBibl.bibl: 1909 [Holiday Leaflets: 8 pp booklets ] York: North Eastern Railway Company Titles include: A break of journey privilege : York ; Bolton Castle : the third act of a tragedy; A forgotten comedy by Sheridan; The King-makers castle by Wensleydale; From Whitehall to Kirby Moorside ; A Pilgrimage to Coxwold; Due north : Hexham and Hadrian's Wall; Written at the sign of the Squirrel ; A famous sea captain of the North

bibl.title: The Chambers of Commerce Year-Book. A directory of places in the United Kingdom where they are instituted, and a record of their aims and work. Supplemented by specially contributed articles and other information. Edited by Charles Eyre Pascoe. vol. 1. Jan. 1908-Jan. 1909.
English Notes for American Acceptance. An unconventional guide-book to the north of England, offered to Americans [illustrated]
bibl.publisher: York: Great Northern & North Eastern Railway Companies

bibl.title: Lovely May and leafy June : a seasonable suggestion: to many thousands of admirers of merrie England
bibl.publisher: (London and Tunbridge Wells) : Photochrome. Co. Ltd.

bibl.title: Dickens in Yorkshire : being notes of a journey to the delightful village of Dotheboys, near Greta Bridge.

div.head: Periodical contributions
div.p: Pascoe was a prolific contributor to magazines, both American and English. Many of these contributions were subsequently recycled as book-length publications. A few titles listed here are not explicitly credited to Pascoe, though there is good reason to believe he was their author. Many titles mentioned elsewhere are yet to be traced. For those which have been found online, a link is provided below.

listBibl.bibl: “An English By-Lane”, New York: Appletons' journal. New Series, Vol 1 (1876) pp 231-233
“In Memoriam: Temple Bar.” Illus. New York: Appletons' journal. vol 2 (1877) pp 75-81
"Life on a canal boat". Unsigned report in The Standard, Sept 6, 1877 [TEI transcript]
"The Canal Boats Act and the Bargemen". Unsigned report in The Standard, Sept 8, 1877 [TEI transcript]
"The Canal Boats Act and the Bargemen". Unsigned report in The Standard, Sept 11, 1877 [TEI transcript]
`Christ’s Hospital’ (“Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour (Mar 1, 1879): issue 1418 pp 135-140.
`The Charterhouse School’ (“Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour (Jul 26, 1879): Issue 1439, pp 471-475.
`St Paul’s School’ (“Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour (Nov 29, 1879): Issue 1457, pp 763-766.
`Merchant Taylors’ School (“Public Schools of England” series) The Leisure hour(Dec 13, 1879): Issue 1459, pp 796-800.
`"First Nights" at London Theatres' The American (27 November 1880), pp 103-104.
`The Story of the English magazines’ Atlantic Monthly, (Sept 1884), vol 64, pp 364-374.
1884 `Buying a house’ The Leisure hour, (May 1884), vol 33, pp 294-298.
`A Ramble round Windsor’ The Leisure hour (Apr 1887), vol 36, pp 251-260.
`The Queen’s Homes. Introductory - the Queen's Household’ The Leisure hour (Jan 1888), vol 37, pp. 16-21.
`The Queen’s Homes. Kensington Palace’ The Leisure hour (Feb 1888), vol 37, pp 115-123.
`The Queen’s Homes: Frogmore House’. Illus Victor Prout. The Leisure Hour (Mar 1888), vol 37, pp 167-174.
`The Queen’s Homes: Claremont’ The Leisure hour (Apr 1888) , vol 37, pp. 237-241.
`The Queen’s Homes: Buckingham Palace’ The Leisure hour (May 1888): , vol 37, pp 317-324.
`Downing Street: the Official Residence of the First Lord of the Treasury. With Illustrations by Philip Norman. The Leisure hour, (Jun 1888) pp 374-384.
`The Queen’s Homes: Windsor Castle’ The Leisure hour (Aug 1888), vol 37, pp 515-524.
`The Queen’s Homes: Osborne’ The Leisure hour (Sep 1888), vol 37, pp 598-604.
`The Queen’s Homes: Balmoral' The Leisure hour (Nov 1888), vol 37, pp 753-759.
`Falmouth: its past present, and future' The Cornish Magazine, (Sept 1898), vol 1, no 3, pp 210 - 219.

div.head: Newspaper correspondence
div.p: Pascoe was also an indefatigable writer of letters to the editor. His letters suggest he held solidly middle class views, intensely patriotic, but tinged with "liberalism". The following letters are available in transcribed form.

listBibl.bibl: 1867 "The Reception of the Belgians." letter to the editor Royal Cornwall Gazette. (May 16, 1867) Issue 3331, p. 6
1884 “American Payments to English Authors” letter in The Athenaeum. (Nov 8, 1884) Issue 2976, p. 592
1886 "The Theatres" [quotes a letter from CEP] Daily News, (June 7, 1886) Issue 12529
1889 “The Maybrick case” Daily News (Aug 13, 1889) Issue 13526, p. 2
1896 “A word for the skipper” Daily News (July 30, 1896) Issue 15706
1896 “A literary Londoner in Liverpool” letter to Liverpool Daily Post, (25 May 1896) p. 7
1896 “Is it fact or flourish of phrases?” letter to Daily News, (Oct 15 1896) Issue 15772, p.3 ]
1899 “The Transvaal Crisis” letters to Daily News Sep 30 1899, p. 6 ; Dec 30 1899, p. 9; Jan 3, 1900, p. 3

bibl.title: [Application to Royal Literary Fund] Charles Eyre Pascoe B 1842
bibl.ref: Archives of the Royal Literary Fund 2739. World Microfilms. Nineteenth Century Collections Online

listBibl.bibl: 1912Barking of Dogs and sudden Death [Report of Pascoe's death from the Sutton and Cheam Advertiser, 15 Nov 1912, page 5.] [TEI transcription]

div.head: Untraced works
div.p: Pascoe's name appears sporadically as author of many items not yet traced. Some of these may be ghostly works that never existed, but it seems fair to assume that the others were simply over looked by library collectors and cataloguers of such ephemera.

cit.quote: The Hearth and Home : The Great Illustrated Home Paper. This number is very Full of Good Things. Part of contents... "Case of Jewels by Charles Eyre Pascoe"

In this connection it gives us pleasure to announce that we have entered into an arrangement with Mr. Charles Eyre Pascoe, of London, whereby he is to furnish for early publication a paper on The Potters and Potteries of Staffordshire. Mr. Pascoe's...

Later [Pascoe] came to England as [the Boston Daily Globe's] “Special Correspondent,” and was also accredited correspondent of Appleton's (New York) Journal, and the American Art Journal, and was appointed the Reviser in England of all the numerous articles of British Biography and Topography appearing in the “American Cyclopaedia,” published in 16 volumes octavo (New York, D. Appleton & company). He was specially deputed to write a series of papers for a periodical Appleton's (New York) Journal, on the Municipal Government of London, and on the English Prisons System; and from 1874 to 1880 was occupied in London in contributing solely to American journals and periodicals on English topics. Among these were articles for the New York Herald on the Marquess of Lorne, when first appointed Governor-General of Canada, and the Lord Chief Justice (Coleridge) when about to visit the United States specially undertaken.
cit.bibl: (Claims made in CEP's application to the Royal Literary Fund in 1906.)

cit.quote: ... such eminent writers as Bret Harte, Miss Harriet A. Spofford, Miss Susan Coolidge, Charles Eyre Pascoe, Miss M. E. W. Sherwood, and Miss Helen S. Conant have been secured as contributors [to new Fashions magazine published by Almy Bigelow & Washburn]

Charles Eyre Pascoe an Englishman who has been visiting America, writes to the London Daily News that the present depression in the States is due not to tariff or money, but to the bicycle craze. The bicycles are a curse to the country :— Every shop girl on $4 or $5 a week, every office boy and all the young counter-skippers must own a bike apiece, and they buy them on the instalment plan, paying $10 down and from $2 to $5 a week until they are paid for, with Interest. The result is that millions and millions of dollars are being withdrawn from the normal course of trade and delivered into this one branch, and other people find it extremely difficult to get money to live on. As England and France also have a bicycle craze and without the extreme business depression that prevails here, this explanation needs to be explained. One would suppose from this theory that the money [rest of article lost]

Fiction in Newspapers wasn’t new when Col Taylor began his experiment which was to revolutionize the newspaper practices of his times; and even in the Globe’s own first weeks—back in 1872 —it had printed a short story called “Love and Madness” by its staff editor Charles Eyre Pascoe. But that had been a mere one-shot.
